activities_projects.title: Methodological Critique of the Poverty Line
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the limitations of income-based poverty measurements
steps: Contrast the 'Minimum Subsistence' approach (calorie-based) with the 'Reasonable Level of Living' approach
Analyze why the urban poverty line (₹1000) is higher than the rural line (₹816) despite lower calorie requirements in cities
Draft a proposal for a 'Multi-dimensional Poverty Index' (MPI) that includes sanitation, fuel, and electricity as indicators
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically assesses the scientific and social validity of poverty estimation
activities_projects.evaluation: Theoretical rigor and logical consistency in the proposal (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Geography of Inequality: Inter-State Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the structural reasons for regional poverty disparities
steps: Compare the 'Public Distribution System' (PDS) efficiency in Andhra Pradesh with the land reform success in West Bengal
Correlate the high poverty ratios in Odisha and Bihar with their historical lack of industrial investment
Argue why human resource development in Kerala led to a faster poverty decline than agricultural growth in other states
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the link between state-level policy and macro-economic outcomes
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparative research and evidentiary support (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Colonialism and the 'De-industrialization' Thesis
activities_projects.objective: Trace the historical underpinnings of chronic poverty in India
steps: Analyze the impact of British 'Tariff Policies' on the Indian handicraft and textile industries
Explain how the shift from food crops to cash crops during the colonial era created a 'vulnerability cycle' for small farmers
Connect the low growth rate of the 1950s (the 'Hindu Rate of Growth') to the colonial economic structure
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes historical path-dependency with modern economic stagnation
activities_projects.evaluation: Historical accuracy and strength of the economic argument (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Vulnerability and Intersectionality Audit
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how social identity compounds economic deprivation
steps: Map the 'Double Burden' of poverty: being a woman in a Scheduled Tribe household vs. a male in a Scheduled Caste household
Analyze the 'Vulnerability' of landless agricultural laborers to climate-induced income shocks
Evaluate why 'Social Exclusion' acts as both a cause and a consequence of poverty
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies the concept of intersectionality to poverty and marginalization
activities_projects.evaluation: Sophistication in identifying layered social disadvantages (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Global Poverty Trends: PPP and Exchange Rates
activities_projects.objective: Understand the complexity of comparing poverty across different currencies
steps: Explain the concept of 'Purchasing Power Parity' (PPP) and why the $1.90/day line is used globally
Compare the poverty reduction velocity of East Asian 'Tiger Economies' (China, Vietnam) with South Asian nations
Analyze why 'Sub-Saharan Africa' remains stagnant in poverty despite global economic growth
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates India's poverty challenges within the global macro-economic landscape
activities_projects.evaluation: Computational accuracy and global contextual awareness (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Anti-Poverty Program Implementation Gap
activities_projects.objective: Conduct a critical audit of government welfare efficiency
steps: Investigate the 'Targeting Error': why many non-poor receive BPL benefits while the deserving poor are excluded
Analyze the 'Implementation Gap' in MGNREGA regarding delayed wage payments and lack of asset creation
Propose a 'Direct Benefit Transfer' (DBT) model to minimize leakages in poverty alleviation schemes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies administrative and systemic hurdles in welfare delivery
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical insight into policy failure and reform (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Debt Trap and Rural Credit Markets
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the financial mechanisms of rural indigence
steps: Model the 'Debt-Trap' of a small farmer: high input costs -> crop failure -> informal high-interest loan -> land alienation
Discuss the role of 'Social Obligations' (festivals/weddings) in pushing poor families deeper into debt
Evaluate the impact of 'Micro-credit' and 'Self-Help Groups' (SHGs) in providing an alternative to moneylenders
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the financial cycle of poverty and the importance of formal credit
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the debt-cycle model and solution feasibility (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Economic Growth vs. Targeted Programs
activities_projects.objective: Debate the two-pronged strategy of the Indian government
steps: Argument A: 'Economic Growth' is the best poverty-alleviation tool (Trickle-down theory)
Argument B: 'Targeted Anti-Poverty Programs' are necessary because growth doesn't reach everyone (Inclusion theory)
Synthesize a conclusion on why a 'Mixed Approach' is required for the Indian context
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes differing economic ideologies on development
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of arguments and ability to reach a nuanced synthesis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Human Poverty: The Dignity and Rights Approach
activities_projects.objective: Move from 'Standard of Living' to 'Quality of Life' frameworks
steps: Analyze the phrase 'Poverty is a denial of human rights'
Investigate the 'Feminization of Poverty'—why women bear the brunt of nutritional and educational deprivation
Draft a 'Dignity Audit' for a slum area, assessing access to voice, political participation, and respect
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects economic scarcity to the broader socio-political framework of human dignity
activities_projects.evaluation: Ethical depth and sociological observation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Master Project: Poverty-Free District Plan
activities_projects.objective: Apply multi-disciplinary knowledge to design a district-level intervention
steps: Select a high-poverty district and identify its primary economic bottleneck (e.g., lack of irrigation, low literacy)
Integrate 'Asset Creation' (through MGNREGA), 'Human Capital' (Health/Education), and 'Market Linkages' into one plan
Define 'Success Metrics' that go beyond the poverty line (e.g., reduction in stunting, increase in female labor participation)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates comprehensive mastery of poverty dynamics and policy design
activities_projects.evaluation: Feasibility, innovation, and holistic nature of the plan (10 marks)
